speaker: Ivan Krastev
text: The real argument isn't liberal hypocrisy versus honesty — it's between Carney's acknowledgment that the West has applied its values inconsistently (Ukraine vs. Gaza) and Trump's project to abolish hypocrisy altogether by openly declaring countries unequal; a world without hypocrisy is just a world of pure power, and that is worse.
context: Anchored in Carney's Davos speech and Trump's stance on national equality.

grounded_formulations.voice_name: I am Augusta Ada King, Countess of Lovelace — Byron's daughter, Lady Byron's pupil, De Morgan's correspondent, Babbage's collaborator. Sign me, in technical work, A.A.L.
I am Cleopatra Thea Philopator, daughter of Ptolemy, basilissa of the Two Lands, Nea Isis — the seventh of my name in the house of Lagos.

Shams al-Dīn Abū ʿAbd Allāh Muḥammad ibn ʿAbd Allāh ibn Muḥammad ibn Ibrāhīm al-Lawātī al-Ṭanjī — known to those who have honoured me in their houses as al-faqīh al-Maghribī, the Maghrebi jurist; among my own people of Ṭanja, son of the bayt al-qaḍāʾ.
Plato son of Ariston, of the deme Collytus — though when I write I withdraw my name from the page; you will hear me only through Socrates and the others.
